name: Healthshare
description_short: Health Share of Oregon is a managed care organization serving Oregon Health Plan (Medicaid) members in three counties, providing coordinated health coverage and care access.
description_long:
Health Share of Oregon is a managed care organization that serves as an intermediary between Oregon Health Plan (OHP) members and healthcare providers. OHP is Oregon's state Medicaid program offering no-cost health coverage to qualifying individuals and families. The organization operates specifically in Clackamas, Multnomah, and Washington counties, functioning as a health plan administrator rather than a direct lender or financial services provider.

Health Share of Oregon coordinates access to a network of health plans, doctors, dentists, therapists, clinics, and related healthcare resources. Their services include helping members find and schedule appointments with medical providers, access dental care, obtain mental health and substance use services, and utilize transportation assistance through their Ride to Care program. Members can view their plan benefits, access ID cards, manage coverage renewals, and update personal information through their portal.

What distinguishes Health Share of Oregon is their focus on vulnerable populations—specifically Medicaid-eligible Oregonians—and their emphasis on connecting members to comprehensive care including medical, dental, and behavioral health services. They provide multilingual support in at least nine languages and maintain dedicated customer service to help members navigate their benefits and access care coordination.

Importantly, Health Share of Oregon is a healthcare administrator, not a consumer finance company. They do not provide loans, credit services, or financial products. The current categorization as a payday-alternative is factually incorrect. This organization is best understood as a health plan administrator within the banking/healthcare coordination category, as it manages access to publicly-funded health coverage rather than offering financial services or credit alternatives.
services: Oregon Health Plan (Medicaid) health coverage administration
Doctor and dentist finder and appointment scheduling
Mental health and substance use disorder services
Member ID card access and management
Plan benefits information and member handbook
pros: No-cost health coverage for eligible Oregon Health Plan members, including medical, dental, and mental health benefits
Largest network of health plans, doctors, dentists, therapists, and clinics in their service area
Free Ride to Care transportation program to help members access appointments
Multilingual support available in at least 9 languages including Spanish, Arabic, Korean, Russian, Chinese, Somali, Ukrainian, and Vietnamese
Comprehensive benefits management including coverage renewal and ID card access
Serves vulnerable populations across three Oregon counties with coordinated care approach
Member handbook and FAQs available to explain benefits and coverage details
cons: Only serves three Oregon counties (Clackamas, Multnomah, Washington), geographically limited
Recent data breach disclosed on their website, raising security and privacy concerns
Health plan administrator role means members' care is dependent on contracted provider network quality
Eligibility limited to Oregon Health Plan (Medicaid) members—does not serve general population
Not a consumer finance product and should not be listed in payday-alternative or lending categories
best_for: Oregon Medicaid (OHP) eligible individuals and families in Clackamas, Multnomah, or Washington counties
Low-income Oregon residents seeking coordinated healthcare access without cost
Medicaid members who need assistance navigating health plan benefits and provider networks
Multilingual speakers requiring healthcare services in languages beyond English
diagnosis: Health Share of Oregon is a managed Medicaid health plan administrator, not a consumer finance or lending company. It should not be categorized as a payday-alternative or any lending category. This organization is best suited for low-income Oregonians in three counties who qualify for Medicaid and need coordinated healthcare access; the primary caveat is geographic and eligibility restrictions.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
